title: Medical Implications of Less-Lethal Weapons Expert Committee: vacancies
body: " Applications are now closed. We’ll review applications and hold interviews in November. If your application is successful, we will let you know.   The Medical Implications of Less-Lethal Weapons Expert Committee (MILLWEC) gives advice to government on the effects of less-lethal weapons on the human body.  MILLWEC vacancies  MILLWEC is recruiting for new members and a new chair.  Members  MILLWEC is looking for people with:   scientific backgrounds: you should have senior-level experience in clinical or academic work, or research – in areas related to how less-lethal weapons affect humans lay backgrounds: you should be able to confidently offer independent views, represent the public’s interest, think critically and make sound judgments   Chair  MILLWEC is looking for someone experienced at serving on committees at a senior level.  About the appointments  Appointment period: Up to 3 years with the possibility of re-appointment if your performance is rated as satisfactory.  Location: Central London or remotely.   Time commitment: Up to 10 to 15 days a year.   Payment: Unpaid, but you can claim for travel and meal costs.   How to apply    Read the recruitment packs for more information on the application process and person specification.   Send your application to millwec.secretariat@homeoffice.gov.uk. You should include:     a CV setting out your career history and any professional qualifications a cover letter including:  which role you’re applying for why you think you’re suitable for the role contact details for 2 referees information about any conflict or potential conflict of interest   a completed diversity monitoring form   The closing date for applications is 3 November 2025, 11:59pm.   The diversity monitoring form is not fully accessible. If you need help to use it email millwec.secretariat@homeoffice.gov.uk.  "
